channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.nintendolife.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.nintendolife.com

item.title: Round Up: The Final Previews Are In For Splatoon Raiders
item.content:encoded:
Here's what the critics had to say

Nintendo's Splatoon Raiders Direct has now aired, and the final previews for this single-player title have officially gone live ahead of the launch on 23rd July 2026.

If you haven't already seen our own "hands-on" here on Nintendo Life, we've got high hopes for this new solo experience:

"If the stages themselves can keep on innovating, coming up with new platforming challenges and more interesting ways to tackle the enemies, then the Splatoon formula may have finally found its true single-player potential. Let's hope it still has some surprises in the tank come launch day."

Here's what other critics have to say about their own time with the game so far:

Game File: "It was very Ratchet & Clank. You know, the Sony PlayStation series of third-person shooters featuring a furry Lombax and his robot/backpack? Again, maybe that doesn’t seem like a compliment? I promise that it is."

Vooks: "Splatoon Raiders so far seems like a good change of scenery. It’s another opportunity to enjoy the world of Splatoon without needing to jump into an online session."

IGN: "Turns out, Splatoon Raiders is kind of a roguelite with persistent progression. Who knew? But it does give you an opportunity to try out all of your wonderful toys and things like Shiver’s Showstopper, which summons a giant rocket-powered shark (yes, really) that blasts through anything unlucky enough to get in your way. And, based on what I played, Raiders seems to be trying to do enough that while your core action – blast through Salmonids and get stuff, rinse and repeat – is the same, things always feel different enough that it stays interesting."

CGMagazine: "Charm, hilarious puns, an endless mess, and challenging encounters await players daring enough to take up residence on the Spirhalite Islands. With the move (and exclusivity) to the Nintendo Switch 2, Splatoon visuals have vastly improved, and I was even able to notice some of the wear and tear on background glass, the Hideout Ship and the Exploration Bot that helps you dig up treasure. Nintendo pulled out all the stops for Splatoon Raiders"

Polygon: "For those who feel overwhelmed by Splatoon 3’s wealth of content, it might be a less intimidating way to get a feel for the series. No matter how it turns out, I wouldn’t be surprised if its buildcrafting ends up having a game-changing impact on Splatoon 4. The tinkering is too good to leave on the sidelines."

So, are you sold on Splatoon Raiders yet? What did you think of the Splatoon Raiders Direct? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/07/round-up-the-final-previews-are-in-for-splatoon-raiders
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Digital Foundry Delivers Its Tech Review Of Sonic Frontiers On Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
"The technical improvements are substantial"

Sonic Frontiers got a not-so-surprising Definitive Edition release on the Switch 2 last week as part of the ongoing Sonic the Hedgehog 35th anniversary celebrations.

Digital Foundry has now taken a closer look at this version of the game, and while it's not the "perfect port", it's a "substantial" improvement on the technical front.

The game now offers two distinct visual modes - with the default setting prioritising 60fps performance and the graphics mode targeting higher fidelity at a mostly "stable" 30fps. In portable mode, there are more frequent dips in the open-world segments, but dynamic resolution scaling makes it a "largely playable" experience, "on the go". As for cutscenes, they mostly remain locked at 30fps.

The resolution in graphics mode runs at a native 1080p resolution, while the performance mode is 720p. "Crucially", the graphics mode retains screen space reflections (SSR) on water, which Digital Foundry describes as the "standout visual flourish from the original Switch release". In handheld, performance mode displays between 480p to 720p, and quality mode is 720p to 1080p.

Other improvements in the Switch 2 version include "higher-quality shadows, better texture filtering, and improved levels of detail" across the open world. Unfortunately, "pop-in" is still apparently "highly distracting" and this version of the game misses out on some "premium" features including motion blur, water ripple physics and high-end global illumination settings.

Of course, there's no upgrade path for owners of the Switch version of Sonic Frontiers. However, existing Switch save data will transfer over the Switch 2 version of the game. The physical Switch 2 release has also been confirmed as a Game-Key Card.

Do you plan on checking out Sonic Frontiers: Definitive Edition on the Switch 2? Let us know in the comments.

[source digitalfoundry.net]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/07/digital-foundry-delivers-its-tech-review-of-sonic-frontiers-on-switch-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Tomb Raider: Definitive Edition Switch 2 Limited Run Physical Release Announced
item.content:encoded:
Pre-orders open this week

Following the shadow drop of Tomb Raider: Definitive Edition on the Switch 2 last November, physical distributor Limited Run Games has now officially announced a physical release.

Pre-orders for a standard edition and collector's edition go live on 2nd July 2026. The standard edition priced at $49.99 comes with a copy of the game and booklet. And the collector's edition for $79.99 includes a copy of the game, soundtrack CD, steelcase, map of Yamatai and pry axe keychain.

If you haven't already played this Lara Croft adventure, you can check out our review here on Nintendo Life. It's also available on the original Switch. More recently, Aspyr released Rise of the Tomb Raider: 20 Year Celebration on the Switch 2.

Would you be interested in this physical copy of Tomb Raider for Switch 2? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Cruis'n Blast Has Been Delisted From The Switch eShop
item.content:encoded:
Speeding away

Back in 2021, the Cruis'n series returned to consoles with the release of the arcade title Cruis'n Blast on the Switch.

Years later, this racing title has now been delisted from the Nintendo eShop. There's no word from the developer Raw Thrills about the game's removal from the digital store.

Fortunately, if you didn't get around to purchasing a digital copy of the title, you might still be able to hunt down a physical copy in select locations.

This latest installment in the series includes licensed vehicles, vehicle customisation, "thrilling" tracks and local split screen. When this title raced onto the Switch, we thought it was a fun ride, even though it lacked online multiplayer:

"As long as you're willing to put up with some performance hits and the fact that there's no online multiplayer, Cruis'n Blast is a hugely entertaining arcade style racer with incredible set-pieces that has us crossing every possible body part in the hope that it's successful enough to encourage more of the same somewhere down the line."

More recently, the Raw Thrills title Fast & Furious: Arcade Edition got a release on the Switch eShop with the help of GameMill Entertainment and Cradle Games.

If we hear any updates about the availability of Cruis'n Blast on the Switch, we'll let you know.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/07/cruisn-blast-has-been-delisted-from-the-switch-eshop
